'''Stuart Littler''' (born 19 February 1979) is the former head coach of [[Oldham RLFC|Oldham]] in [[RFL League 1]]. He is a former [[Ireland national rugby league team|Ireland]] international [[rugby league]] footballer and a former head coach of [[Ireland national rugby league team|Ireland]].

Littler played for the [[Salford Red Devils|Salford City Reds]] in the [[Super League]], playing for his hometown club, [[Leigh Centurions]] and finishing his career with [[Swinton Lions]], retiring at the end of the 2016 season.

Littler's position of choice was as a {{rlp|ce}}. He could also operate in the {{rlp|sr}} and also played some games on the {{rlp|wg}}.

==Background==
Littler was born in [[Higher End]], [[Metropolitan Borough of Wigan|Wigan]], [[Greater Manchester]], England. He is of Irish descent.

He is a lifelong resident of [[Leigh, Greater Manchester|Leigh]].

==Career==
Littler is an [[Ireland national rugby league team|Ireland]] international.

Littler was expected to leave the club following [[Salford Red Devils|Salford]]'s relegation to [[RFL Championship|National League One]]. Littler was expected to seal a move to [[London Broncos#2006–2011: Harlequins Rugby League|Harlequins RL]], however Chairman [[Ian Lenagan]] decided not to offer him a deal.

Littler joined Salford in 1998 – his first professional club and is equal top-try scorer in a season for the club, alongside [[David Watkins (rugby)|David Watkins]].

He was named in the [[Ireland national rugby league team#2008 World Cup Training Squad|Ireland training squad]] for the [[2008 Rugby League World Cup]],<ref name="Ireland Name World Cup 40 Man Training Squad">{{cite web|url=http://www.rli.ie/story.php?id=53|title=Ireland Name World Cup 40 Man Training Squad|publisher=Rugby League Ireland|date=2008-08-01|accessdate=2008-08-02}}{{dead link|date=December 2017 |bot=InternetArchiveBot |fix-attempted=yes }}</ref> and the [[2008 Rugby League World Cup squads#Ireland|Ireland]] squad for the [[2008 Rugby League World Cup]].<ref name="Cassidy included in Ireland squad">{{cite web|url=http://news.bbc.co.uk/sport1/hi/rugby_league/7657596.stm|title=Cassidy included in Ireland squad|publisher=BBC|date=2008-10-07|accessdate=2008-10-07}}</ref>

In October and November 2014, Stuart played in the [[2014 European Cup (rugby league)|2014 European Cup]] competition.

In 2016, he was called up to the [[Ireland national rugby league team|Ireland]] squad for the [[2017 Rugby League World Cup qualification – Europe#Group B|2017 Rugby League World Cup European Pool B qualifiers]].<ref name="Warrington Wolves trio named in Ireland squad for World Cup qualifiers">{{cite web|url=http://www.warringtonguardian.co.uk/sport/14763089.Warrington_Wolves_trio_named_in_Ireland_squad_for_World_Cup_qualifiers|title=Warrington Wolves trio named in Ireland squad for World Cup qualifiers|publisher=Warrington Guardian|date=24 September 2016|accessdate=26 September 2016}}</ref>

==Coaching==
===Swinton Lions===
After retiring with Swinton he remained with the club as an assistant coach under head coach [[John Duffy (rugby league)|John Duffy]]. When Duffy left the club in July 2017, he stepped into the head coach's shoes commencing with an away match at [[Bradford Bulls]]' [[Odsal Stadium]] on 23 July 2017.
He parted company with Swinton in July 2021.<ref>[https://www.seriousaboutrl.com/championship-club-sack-head-coach-41548/%3ffbclid=IwAR1PXKFajeOIstEKP4P3AOLhLsKf6OhFnio6oO9aHqHntq_zYe4ASYFybs0&amp SARL - Championship club sack head coach]</ref>
===Oldham RLFC===

On 6 October 2021, he was reported as the new head coach for [[Oldham RLFC]].<ref>{{cite web|website=Love Rugby League|date=6 Oct 2021|accessdate=6 Oct 2021|title=Ireland boss Stuart Littler is back in club coaching|url=https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/ireland-boss-stuart-littler-is-back-in-club-coaching/}}</ref>

On 1 Aug 2023 it was reported that Oldham had announced his departure amid speculation that he was about to take up the vacant [[Widnes Vikings]] head coach role.<ref>{{cite web|website=Love Rugby League|date=1 Aug 2023|accessdate=1 Aug 2023|title=Widnes Vikings: League 1 coach ‘relieved of position’ amid speculation with vacant Championship job|url=https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/widnes-vikings-league-1-coach-relieved-of-position-amid-speculation-with-vacant-championship-job}}</ref>
